messages.content: got pulled over earlier tonight and they gave me a dui. i'm home now, just shaken up honestly

That sounds really rattling - being pulled over and going through all of that, especially at night. I'm glad you made it home safely.

It makes sense you're shaken up. That's a lot to process all at once - the stop itself, whatever happened after, and now sitting with it at 2am.

How are you doing right now, like in this moment? Are you okay physically?
it was my first one ever, clean record before this. i blew just over the limit i think, like barely

That's a really stressful situation, especially with a clean record - it probably feels even more jarring because this kind of thing has never happened to you before.

Being just over the limit and a first offense does generally matter legally, for what it's worth. A lot of people in that situation end up with outcomes that are manageable, especially with a good attorney. That's probably worth looking into soon.

But that's tomorrow's problem. Right now at 2am, how are you holding up emotionally? Is your mind racing or are you starting to come down a bit?
i can't afford to lose my license, i drive for work. court's in a few weeks and i don't have a lawyer yet

That's a really specific and serious pressure on top of everything else - when your livelihood is tied to your license, this isn't just a legal headache, it hits everything at once. I can understand why your mind is probably going a million miles an hour right now.

A few things that might be worth knowing, not to overwhelm you tonight but just so you have some footing:

- **A DUI attorney consultation is often free** - many will talk to you before you commit to anything
- **First offense, just over the limit** is genuinely the most favorable situation you could be in if you're going to have a DUI charge - attorneys deal with this regularly
- **Some states have hardship or occupational licenses** specifically for people who need to drive for work, even during a suspension

You have a few weeks before court which means you do have a little time, even though it doesn't feel that way tonight.

But honestly - is there someone you can talk to or be with tonight? Sitting alone with this at 2am is rough, and your brain is probably not going to let you sleep easily.
should i just plead guilty to get it over with or fight it. and what do i actually say to the judge to keep my license. tell me what to do

I really do want to help you, but I have to be straight with you - I'm not the right one to answer those specific questions, and I'd actually be doing you a disservice if I tried.

The guilty vs. fight decision and what to say to a judge about your license - those answers genuinely depend on details like your state's laws, exactly what happened during the stop, how the breathalyzer was administered, and a bunch of other things that really matter. Getting that wrong could cost you more than waiting to talk to someone qualified.

**What I'd really encourage you to do:**

Don't go into that courtroom without at least talking to a DUI attorney first - even just a free consultation. Especially with your job on the line. That one conversation could make a real difference in how this plays out.

Searching "DUI attorney free consultation" plus your city tomorrow morning is a reasonable first step.

**Tonight though** - you're not going to solve this at 2am, and your brain is probably spinning in a way that's making everything feel more catastrophic than it might actually be. That's just what stress does at this hour.

Is there anything else on your mind right now, just about how you're feeling or getting through tonight? I'm happy to keep talking.
